_meta: MP-02 motion vocabulary. The compiler emits animation from these entries instead of literal @keyframes, and the reasoning backend may only SELECT ids from this catalog — never invent timing or curves. Durations are seconds. All patterns are transform/opacity/clip-path only so a seeking renderer stays deterministic.

eases._meta: Named curves. A scene must use at least 3 distinct eases so motion does not read as one mechanical sweep.

entrance._meta: One per scene. Offset delays the entrance slightly — a zero-delay entrance reads as a jump cut.

fade-rise.name: Fade rise
fade-rise.description: Opacity in with a short upward travel. The neutral default.
fade-rise.keyframes: from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(16px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }

slide-in-left.name: Slide in from left
slide-in-left.description: Enters from the left edge. Suits stage and split layouts.
slide-in-left.keyframes: from { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-48px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); }

slide-in-right.name: Slide in from right
slide-in-right.description: Mirror of slide-in-left for the opposing panel.
slide-in-right.keyframes: from { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(48px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); }

pop-in.name: Pop in
pop-in.description: Scales up past its resting size. High energy; use for hooks and CTAs.
pop-in.keyframes: from { opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.82); } to { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }

scale-settle.name: Scale settle
scale-settle.description: Subtle scale down into place. Calmer than pop-in.
scale-settle.keyframes: from { opacity: 0; transform: scale(1.06); } to { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }

wipe-reveal.name: Wipe reveal
wipe-reveal.description: Clip-path wipe from the leading edge. Editorial feel.
wipe-reveal.keyframes: from { opacity: 0; clip-path: inset(0 100% 0 0); } to { opacity: 1; clip-path: inset(0 0 0 0); }

bubble-appear.name: Bubble appear
bubble-appear.description: Chat-bubble entrance with a slight rise. Suits vtuber and dialogue scenes.
bubble-appear.keyframes: from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(12px) scale(0.94); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0) scale(1); }

blur-focus.name: Blur focus
blur-focus.description: Resolves from soft to sharp. Reserve for openings.
blur-focus.keyframes: from { opacity: 0; filter: blur(12px); transform: scale(1.03); } to { opacity: 1; filter: blur(0); transform: scale(1); }

midscene._meta: At least two per scene. Without these a scene is a static slide with an entrance — the single most common reason generated video looks like a slide deck.

breathe.name: Breathe
breathe.description: Slow vertical float that keeps a held frame alive.
breathe.keyframes: from { transform: translateY(0); } to { transform: translateY(-8px); }

ken-burns.name: Ken Burns
ken-burns.description: Slow push on imagery. 1 → 1.04, never more.
ken-burns.keyframes: from { transform: scale(1); } to { transform: scale(1.04); }

drift.name: Horizontal drift
drift.keyframes: from { transform: translateX(0); } to { transform: translateX(-14px); }

pulse-accent.name: Accent pulse
pulse-accent.description: Opacity pulse on accent marks. Draws the eye without moving layout.

stagger-lines.name: Line stagger
stagger-lines.description: Sequential reveal of list items. Per-item delay is applied by the compiler.
stagger-lines.keyframes: from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(10px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }

underline-sweep.name: Underline sweep
underline-sweep.description: Rule that draws itself under a headline.
underline-sweep.keyframes: from { transform: scaleX(0); transform-origin: left; } to { transform: scaleX(1); transform-origin: left; }

bar-fill.name: Bar fill
bar-fill.keyframes: from { transform: scaleX(0); transform-origin: left; } to { transform: scaleX(1); transform-origin: left; }

glow-cycle.name: Glow cycle
glow-cycle.description: Soft shadow breathing on panels. Subtle depth cue.
glow-cycle.keyframes: from { box-shadow: 0 0 0 rgba(0,0,0,0); } to { box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0,0,0,0.28); }

role_defaults._meta: Deterministic assignment when no direction is drafted. Every role gets an entrance plus two mid-scene layers so the default output is never a static slide.

transitions._meta: Hard cuts are the default. A shader on every cut is the video equivalent of bolding every word.
